Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The speaker uses the words cover-up.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The president walks out.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: A political drama played out in Washington today, as President Trump demanded that congressional

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Democrats give up on investigations and any consideration of an impeachment inquiry.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: House Speaker Nancy Pelosi did not call for impeachment proceedings today, as she left a closed-door

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: meeting with House Democrats.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: But she had a firm message.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And we believe that the president of the United States is engaged in a cover-up.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Hours later, in the Rose Garden, President Trump reacted.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I don't do cover-ups.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: He accused Pelosi and Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer of, in effect, bad faith before

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: what was supposed to be a White House meeting about infrastructure.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And added, he won't work with Democrats on that issue or anything else, until investigations

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: into him ceased.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I want to do infrastructure.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: But you know what?

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: You can't do it under these circumstances.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So get these phony investigations over with.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Back at the Capitol, Pelosi and Schumer responded.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: He just took a pass, and it just makes me wonder why he did that.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: In any event, I pray for the president of the United States.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: We are interested in doing infrastructure.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: It's clear the president isn't.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: He is looking for every excuse.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: On impeachment, House Democrats are split, some like California's Maxine Waters, who's

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: part of the leadership, favorite.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I'm foreign impeachment.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I've always been foreign impeachment.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I've never backed up.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I've never changed my mind.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I think he should be impeached.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: But others like House Oversight share Elijah Cummings of Maryland are less eager.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Americans are state of course.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Let's continue to look to the courts to provide the remedies with regard to getting us the

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: information that we need to do our jobs.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The Trump administration is pushing back on multiple investigations, including requests

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: by Congress for the president's tax returns.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The Washington Post reported yesterday that a confidential internal revenue service memo

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: says tax returns must be given to Congress unless executive privilege is invoked.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Before the House Financial Services Committee today, Treasury Secretary Stephen Manuchin said

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: the memo doesn't contradict the Treasury's reason for withholding the returns.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: He argues the release request does not have a legislative purpose.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: That memo I understand is addressing a different issue and is not addressing the issue that we

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: in the Department of Justice looked at.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: In New York, meanwhile, state lawmakers passed a bill that would give Congress access to

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: the president's state tax returns if the IRS does not release them in full.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And there was one area of cooperation between Congress and the executive branch.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The House Intelligence Committee says it reached a deal with the Justice Department to turn

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: over some underlying intelligence documents from the Mueller report.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Until later, a federal judge in New York moved swiftly in a ruling to uphold Congress's

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: power to subpoena financial records from banks doing business with the president.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And our Congressional correspondent, Lisa Desjardins, joins me now.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Lisa, you've been reporting on this all day.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: What do we know about what happened at that meeting at the White House?

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: The best route we've gotten so far is from Democrats who are there.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And what Democrats say happened is the president walked in.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He spoke to them for exactly three minutes.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And he's told them that he wants to do infrastructure.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He also wants to deal on trade and some other items.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: But that given Speaker Pelosi's comments today, which he called terrible, accusing him of

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: a cover-up, and the Democratic investigations, he says it's not possible, and he cannot work

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: with them.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He walked out before they could sit down, and that was the end of the meeting.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So we're now, we're reporting on this Democratic leader saying they're not prepared to begin

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: an impeachment inquiry, but they do have some kind of plan.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So what are you hearing?

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: That was the news from before the meeting with the president.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Here's what happened after Democrats met this morning.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Speaker Pelosi said this is what they are doing now.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Democrats seem to generally be on board this idea for now.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: So here's what's happening.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: There are five House Democratic committees that are investigating the president in various

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: areas, some include the Mueller report.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Others include his financial dealings.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: There are planning.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: They will be having hearings, subpoenas being issued, and if those subpoenas are not complied

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: with, as we are seeing so far, they have not been, Democrats are counting on court action.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Now they are considering what's called inherent contempt of Congress.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: That is a power that Congress has on its own to arrest someone even, or issue fines for

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: those who do not cooperate with their investigations.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: It has not been used since 1935.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Speaker Pelosi told members today that it is on the table.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: The message here, Judy, is have faith in the process that we've begun, even as some members

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: would like to go farther now.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Pelosi was able to bring down the temperature in the room, and these court action today especially

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: helps her argument for this longer process.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So that's what the leaders are saying.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: How are Republicans, Democrats, down the line responding to all this?

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: These two stories are highly connected.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Of course, the president is saying that the investigations are the reasons that he will

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: not cooperate with Democrats.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Democrats say that they think that this exposes a weak point on the part of the president.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: They also believe Democrats that this was a setup that the president was not able to deal

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: on infrastructure because the truth is it is a difficult issue for Republicans to pay for

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: a $2 trillion infrastructure deal.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Democrats believe the president went into that meeting looking for an excuse to get out

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: of the infrastructure deal he had agreed to tentatively before.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Republicans, on the other hand, say it's Nancy Pelosi, who was voting the president, when

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: she said that he's involved in a cover-up, a crime, very strong words.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Some Republicans, though, Judy are having a difficult time with this.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: I spoke to John Cornyn of Texas, the senator, who said I understand why the president feels

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: as he does, but we do have a duty to do our jobs.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And he's hopeful that the president actually engages on at least some topics soon.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: That remains to be seen.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Well, speaking of that, I mean, the president is saying I'm not going to talk to you about

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: anything.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: We're not going to negotiate or anything as long as you are investigating me, thinking about

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: impeachment.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So what real world effect do they think this is going to have right now?

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: I think this is the most important question.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: There are many issues really kind of ready for action in Congress right now in meeting action.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Let's look at a few.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: First of all, hurricane, fire, and flood relief, much of that from last year, billions of

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: dollars waiting for action.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: There had hoped to be a deal this week.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: It looks like that may be slipping away.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: There are also approaching budget cuts later this year, and there was a potential deal this

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: week also getting toward a deal in that.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: That seems to also be slipping away.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: There also is a lot of talk about doing something on healthcare and prescription drug costs

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: on a bipartisan way.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: But if the president is not engaging on these topics, it creates a problem for everyone.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Some important reporting from our Yamiche Alcindor White House aides told her that the president

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: will engage on some things like, for example, keeping government open.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: But I think we need to see day by day how the president handles this, how he deals with Democrats

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: today.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He didn't want to deal with them at all.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So interesting and fast-moving Lisa Desjardins.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Thank you.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: You're welcome.
